# zevaro — Codebase Audit

**Audit Date:** 2026-05-02T02:54:45Z
**Branch:** main
**Commit:** f8e9cf17acfca8f44e620e15d4b10c9b2b4d255c ZV-003: Add OpenAPI 3.1 spec, generated docs, and tooling
**Auditor:** Claude Code (Automated)
**Purpose:** Zero-context reference for AI-assisted development
**Audit File:** zevaro-Audit.md
**Scorecard:** zevaro-Scorecard.md
**OpenAPI Spec:** openapi.yaml (at repo root — generated by ZV-003)

> This audit is the source of truth for the zevaro codebase structure, models, services, and configuration.
> The OpenAPI spec (openapi.yaml) is the source of truth for all endpoints, DTOs, and API contracts.
> An AI reading this audit + the OpenAPI spec should be able to generate accurate code
> changes, new features, tests, and fixes without filesystem access.

> **Baseline state:** Zevaro is a pre-alpha skeleton. ZV-001 produced the directory structure,
> build pipeline, and Wails app shell. ZV-002 verified §3.1 alignment. ZV-003 produced openapi.yaml
> and tooling. No application logic (handlers, models, services, repositories) has been implemented yet.
> Sections covering unimplemented layers explicitly state their status and the task that implements them.

---
## 1. Project Identity

```
Project Name:           Zevaro
Repository URL:         https://github.com/zevaro/zevaro
Primary Language:       Go 1.22+ (daemon) + TypeScript 5 / React 18 (GUI via Wails v2)
Framework:              Wails v2 (Go backend + OS webview frontend)
Language/Runtime:       go1.26.2 darwin/arm64 | Node v25.6.1
Build Tool:             Makefile + Wails CLI + goreleaser
Package Manager:        Go modules (go.mod) + pnpm (frontend)
Current Branch:         main
Latest Commit Hash:     f8e9cf17acfca8f44e620e15d4b10c9b2b4d255c
Latest Commit Message:  ZV-003: Add OpenAPI 3.1 spec, generated docs, and tooling
Audit Timestamp:        2026-05-02T02:54:45Z
```

```
./.claude/settings.local.json

./Zevaro-Roadmap.md

**Summary:** Single Go module at `github.com/zevaro/zevaro`. Daemon entry point is `cmd/zevaro/`. Internal packages under `internal/` — currently only `internal/app/` (Wails lifecycle) and `internal/telemetry/` (intentional sentinel). Frontend lives in `frontend/src/`. All future domain packages (server, api, providers, routing, storage, etc.) will be added to `internal/` per §3.1 of Zevaro-Architecture.md. The `openapi.yaml` at the repo root is the canonical API contract.

### Build commands (Makefile)

```
Build:    make build          (frontend-build → go build -tags desktop)
Test:     make test           (test-go + test-frontend)
Lint:     make lint           (lint-go + lint-frontend + lint-openapi)
Docs:     make docs           (redocly build-docs openapi.yaml → api/openapi/docs/index.html)
Dev:      make dev            (wails dev — hot-reload)
Clean:    make clean
Release:  goreleaser release  (via .goreleaser.yaml — implemented in ZV-057)
```

## 4. Configuration & Infrastructure Summary

### Configuration files

| File | Key facts |
|---|---|
| `Makefile` | Build orchestration — targets: build, test, lint, lint-go, lint-frontend, lint-openapi, docs, dev, clean |
| `.golangci.yml` | Strict Go linter config — enables: errcheck, govet, staticcheck, revive, gosec, gocyclo, gocritic, misspell, unparam, unconvert, ineffassign, prealloc, forbidigo. Bans fmt.Print/log.Print/log.Fatal/log.Panic. Min cyclomatic complexity: 20. |
| `.goreleaser.yaml` | Cross-platform release config — targets darwin/linux/windows (amd64+arm64). Produces tar.gz/zip archives + checksums.txt. Release is a GitHub draft. |
| `.redocly.yaml` | Redocly OpenAPI lint config — no-unused-components rule disabled (schemas are defined; endpoints reference them; rule will be re-enabled as paths are validated). |
| `wails.json` | Wails v2 project manifest — name: zevaro, frontend install: pnpm install, build: pnpm run build, dev watcher: pnpm run dev. |
| `frontend/tsconfig.json` | TypeScript strict mode — target ES2020, strict: true, noUnusedLocals, noUnusedParameters, noFallthroughCasesInSwitch, noEmit. |
| `frontend/vite.config.ts` | Vite config — React plugin, Vitest config (environment: jsdom, setupFiles: test-setup.ts). |
| `frontend/tailwind.config.js` | Tailwind — darkMode via class/data-theme attribute, extends theme with CSS variable-based colors from §5 palette. |
| `frontend/eslint.config.js` | ESLint — TypeScript parser, react + react-hooks plugins, no-console: warn, @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars: error. |
| `.editorconfig` | Editor conventions — lf line endings, UTF-8, Go uses tabs, TS/JS/JSON/YAML use 2-space indent. |
| `.gitignore` | Standard — ignores build/, vendor/, node_modules/, coverage.out, frontend/dist/, zevaro.db, config.yaml, pnpm artifacts in frontend/. |

**Runtime config (`config.yaml`):** Not yet present at runtime. The config system (ZV-006) will read from `~/Library/Application Support/Zevaro/config.yaml` (macOS) / `~/.local/share/zevaro/config.yaml` (Linux) / `%LOCALAPPDATA%\Zevaro\config.yaml` (Windows). No config.yaml file is committed to the repo (gitignored).

### Connection map

```
Database:        SQLite — platform data directory (not yet implemented — ZV-007)
                 macOS:   ~/Library/Application Support/Zevaro/zevaro.db
                 Linux:   ~/.local/share/zevaro/zevaro.db
                 Windows: %LOCALAPPDATA%\Zevaro\zevaro.db
Cache:           In-process semantic cache (not yet implemented — ZV-027)
Message Broker:  None — no message broker used
External APIs:   Cloud AI providers (Anthropic, OpenAI, xAI, Google, etc.) — not yet implemented
                 Update manifest endpoint: https://zevaro.ai/manifest (not yet implemented — ZV-053)
                 Connectivity canary: https://zevaro.ai/connectivity (not yet implemented — ZV-036)
Cloud Services:  None
```

### CI/CD

No CI/CD pipelines. This project deliberately operates without CI. All verification runs locally via `make` targets. Releases run locally via `make release` (ZV-057). See AGENTS.md Absolute Rule 10 and CONVENTIONS.md "No CI/CD Infrastructure".

## 5. Startup & Runtime Behavior

### Entry point

`cmd/zevaro/main.go` — `func main()`

### Startup sequence

1. `newLogger()` — constructs a `*slog.Logger`. Uses `slog.NewJSONHandler` if `ZEVARO_ENV=production`; otherwise `slog.NewTextHandler`. Sets as default slog logger via `slog.SetDefault`.
2. `app.New()` — constructs the Wails `App` struct (currently holds only a `context.Context` field).
3. `wails.Run(&options.App{...})` — starts the Wails event loop with:
   - Window: 1280×800, min 1024×680, background `#0b0d10` (canvas color per §5)
   - `AssetServer`: embeds `frontend/dist/` via `//go:embed all:frontend/dist` (desktop build tag) or empty FS (dev mode)
   - `OnStartup`: `zevaroApp.OnStartup(ctx)` — stores Wails context in `App.ctx`
   - `Bind`: `zevaroApp` — exposes exported App methods to TypeScript
4. On error: logs via slog and calls `os.Exit(1)`

### What is NOT yet initialized on startup

The following subsystems are documented in the architecture but not yet implemented at this phase:
- HTTP daemon (chi router, management API, OpenAI/Anthropic surfaces) — ZV-005
- Config system (config.yaml reading, env overrides) — ZV-006
- Storage layer (GORM + SQLite, AutoMigrate) — ZV-007
- Provider registry — ZV-008
- All routing logic, caching, privacy, MCP gateway, plugins — Phases 3–7

### Background tasks

None yet. Periodic tasks (local model discovery every 30s, key health refresh, cache TTL sweep, auto-update check hourly) are defined in the architecture but not yet implemented.

### Health check

`GET /healthz` is defined in `openapi.yaml` and documented in §3.5. The handler is not yet implemented — ZV-005.

### GUI

Renders a "Hello Zevaro" splash screen at skeleton stage. Full GUI implemented in ZV-041 through ZV-048.

## 6. Data Model / Entity Layer

**No domain entities or GORM models implemented at this stage.**

The storage layer (GORM v2 + modernc.org/sqlite) is implemented in ZV-007. Domain entities are added alongside their owning features in subsequent phases. The database schema is designed per §3.2 of Zevaro-Architecture.md and fully reflected in `openapi.yaml` component schemas.

**Planned tables (per §3.2):**

| Table | Phase | Task |
|---|---|---|
| `providers` | Phase 5 | ZV-019 |
| `provider_keys` | Phase 5 | ZV-019 |
| `models` | Phase 5 | ZV-019 |
| `routing_rules` | Phase 4 | ZV-015 |
| `policy_bundles` | Phase 4 | ZV-017 |
| `project_configs` | Phase 7 | ZV-029 |
| `sessions` | Phase 6 | ZV-023 |
| `requests` | Phase 6 | ZV-023 |
| `request_tags` | Phase 6 | ZV-023 |
| `requests_fts` (FTS5) | Phase 6 | ZV-025 |
| `cache_entries` | Phase 7 | ZV-027 |
| `budgets` | Phase 7 | ZV-031 |
| `mcp_servers` | Phase 7 | ZV-032 |
| `mcp_tools` | Phase 7 | ZV-032 |
| `redaction_patterns` | Phase 7 | ZV-028 |
| `audit_log_exports` | Phase 7 | ZV-037 |
| `plugin_registrations` | Phase 7 | ZV-034 |
| `key_health` | Phase 7 | ZV-035 |

Schema will be managed by GORM AutoMigrate during development; switched to versioned migrations (golang-migrate) for production per CONVENTIONS.md.

## 7. Enum / Constant Inventory

**No Go enums or constants modules implemented yet** — the `internal/constants/` package is defined in the architecture (§3.1, §8) but not yet created (ZV-005 will add the first constants).

**Planned constant categories (per §3.1 and §8):**

- Duration constants: scan interval (30s), key health refresh (5min), cache TTL default (7 days), auto-update check interval (1h)
- Size constants: max queue size (100), port defaults (39237, 11434, 1234, 8080, 8000)
- Enum-like provider IDs: `anthropic`, `openai`, `xai`, `google`, `deepseek`, `mistral`, `groq`, `together`, `cohere`, `ollama`, `lmstudio`, `llamacpp`, `vllm`, `bigpickle`
- Capability flags: `chat`, `embeddings`, `images`, `streaming`, `tools`, `vision`, `audio_transcription`
- Routing strategies: `manual`, `project`, `tag`, `rule`, `auto`, `default`
- Budget modes: `soft_warning`, `hard_cutoff`
- Cache invalidation causes: `ttl`, `manual_purge`, `tag_excluded`, `none`

All enum values are fully specified in `openapi.yaml` as `enum` arrays on their respective schema properties.

**Frontend constants:** `frontend/src/lib/constants.ts` is planned (per §8) but not yet created.

## 8. Data Access / Repository Layer

**No repository or data-access layer implemented at this stage.** The storage layer is implemented in ZV-007, and domain repositories are added alongside their owning features.

When implemented, data access will be inline GORM queries in service/handler code (not a separate repository abstraction layer), consistent with Go idioms. GORM v2 with modernc.org/sqlite (pure-Go driver — no CGo required for the data layer) is the chosen ORM.

**Planned data access patterns:**
- GORM `db.Find`, `db.First`, `db.Create`, `db.Save`, `db.Delete` with struct-tag-driven mapping
- SQLite FTS5 virtual table for prompt history full-text search (raw SQL via GORM `db.Raw`)
- Transaction helpers in `internal/storage/db.go` (ZV-007)

To be added by Phase 2 (ZV-007) and subsequent feature tasks.

## 9. Service / Business Logic Layer — Full Method Signatures

**No service or business logic layer implemented at this stage.**

### Currently implemented: internal/app (Wails lifecycle only)

```
=== App (file: internal/app/app.go) ===
Dependencies: none (context.Context stored at startup)

Public Methods:
  - New() *App
    Purpose: Constructs a new Wails App struct ready to be bound to Wails.
    Calls: nothing
    Throws: nothing
    Transactional: NO

  - OnStartup(ctx context.Context)
    Purpose: Stores the Wails runtime context for the application lifetime.
    Calls: nothing
    Throws: nothing
    Transactional: NO
```

### Future service packages (not yet implemented)

Services will be added to `internal/` sub-packages alongside their feature tasks:

## 10. Controller / Handler / Route Layer — Method Signatures Only

**No HTTP handlers implemented at this stage.** The full API surface is defined in `openapi.yaml` (62 operations across three surfaces). Handlers are implemented in:

- ZV-005 — `/healthz` + HTTP server foundation + middleware
- ZV-010 — OpenAI-compatible surface (`/v1/chat/completions`, `/v1/completions`, `/v1/embeddings`, `GET /v1/models`, `/v1/images/generations`, `/v1/audio/transcriptions`)
- ZV-011 — Anthropic-compatible surface (`POST /v1/messages`, `POST /v1/messages/count_tokens`)
- ZV-005 through management endpoint tasks — `/api/v1/...` management surface (providers, models, routes, policies, projects, sessions, history, spend, budgets, cache, compare, mcp, audit, sharing, discovery, health/keys, plugins, updater)

**Routing framework:** chi v5 (`github.com/go-chi/chi/v5`) — not yet in go.mod; added by ZV-005.

**Wire format:** All request/response shapes are fully specified in `openapi.yaml`. Go handlers will be validated against the spec.

## 11. Security Configuration

**No HTTP security middleware implemented at this stage.** Security is designed per the architecture and `openapi.yaml` security schemes.

```
Authentication:     LocalBearer (bearer token, HTTP scheme)
                    Generated on first launch; stored in OS keychain (Keychain/DPAPI/secret-service)
                    Auto-injected by the GUI; CLI consumers read from ZEVARO_TOKEN env var
                    NOT YET IMPLEMENTED — ZV-005

Token issuer:       Internal — generated locally, stored in OS keychain
Password hashing:   N/A — no user password authentication (local daemon, keychain-based)

Public endpoints (no auth required):
  - POST /v1/chat/completions     (OpenAI-compatible surface)
  - POST /v1/completions          (OpenAI-compatible surface)
  - POST /v1/embeddings           (OpenAI-compatible surface)
  - GET  /v1/models               (OpenAI-compatible surface)
  - POST /v1/images/generations   (OpenAI-compatible surface)
  - POST /v1/audio/transcriptions (OpenAI-compatible surface)
  - POST /v1/messages             (Anthropic-compatible surface)
  - POST /v1/messages/count_tokens (Anthropic-compatible surface)
  - GET  /healthz                 (liveness check)

Protected endpoints (LocalBearer required):
  - /api/v1/**                    (all management endpoints — 53 operations)

CORS:          Configured for localhost-only access (not yet implemented — ZV-005)
CSRF:          N/A — local daemon with bearer token auth; no cookie-based sessions
Rate limiting: Budget guardrails (hard_cutoff mode) serve as application-layer rate limiting
               IP/connection-level rate limiting: not planned (local daemon, single user)

Provider API keys: Encrypted at rest using OS keychain (Keychain on macOS, DPAPI on Windows,
                   secret-service on Linux). Never logged, never returned in API responses.
                   Obfuscated aliases returned by GET /api/v1/health/keys only.
```

## 12. Custom Security Components

**No custom security components implemented at this stage.**

Planned security components (ZV-005):

```
=== LocalBearerMiddleware (file: internal/server/middleware/ — not yet created) ===
Type: chi middleware
Purpose: Validates the LocalBearer token on all /api/v1/* routes.
Extracts credentials from: Authorization header (Bearer scheme)
Validates via: Constant-time comparison against token stored in OS keychain
Sets user context: YES — stores authenticated flag in request context

=== Token generator (file: internal/server/ — not yet created) ===
Purpose: Generates a cryptographically random token on first launch using crypto/rand.
Stores via: OS keychain abstraction (platform-specific).
```

The `internal/telemetry/` package exists as an intentional sentinel (doc.go only) documenting the permanent absence of telemetry. No security components live there.

## 13. Exception / Error Handling

**No global error handler implemented at this stage.** Error handling is designed per the architecture and openapi.yaml error schemas.

**Planned error handling (ZV-005):**

```
=== Global panic recovery (file: internal/server/middleware/ — not yet created) ===
Mechanism: chi middleware — recover() + structured log + 500 response

Error Mappings (per openapi.yaml):
  Management surface (/api/v1/...):
    - Not found     → 404 { "code": "not_found", "message": "..." }
    - Validation    → 400 { "code": "invalid_request", "message": "..." }
    - Unauthorized  → 401 { "code": "unauthorized", "message": "..." }
    - Internal      → 500 { "code": "internal_error", "message": "..." }

  OpenAI surface (/v1/...):
    - Errors        → { "error": { "message": "...", "type": "...", "code": null, "param": null } }
    - Rate limit    → 429 (same shape)
    - Service down  → 503 (same shape)

  Anthropic surface (/v1/messages):
    - Errors        → { "type": "error", "error": { "type": "...", "message": "..." } }
    - Rate limit    → 429 (same shape)
    - Service down  → 503 (same shape)

Standard error response format — management surface:
{
  "code": "<machine_readable_code>",
  "message": "<human_readable>",
  "details": null | { ... }
}
```

**Error philosophy (per §4):** Sentinel errors (`var ErrXxx = errors.New(...)`) for distinguishable failure modes. Wrapped errors (`fmt.Errorf("...: %w", err)`). Error messages never contain user content.

## 14. Mappers / Data Transformation

**No dedicated mapper layer implemented at this stage.**

Zevaro's wire-format translation between OpenAI and Anthropic shapes is planned for:
- `internal/normalize/messages/` — cross-format message normalization (ZV-013)
- `internal/normalize/tools/` — OpenAI ↔ Anthropic tool-call translation (ZV-013)
- `internal/normalize/errors/` — provider-specific → canonical error mapping (ZV-019)

**Serialization approach:** Go `encoding/json` with struct field tags (`json:"field_name"`). No separate mapper layer — providers translate between their wire format and canonical types in `internal/providers/types.go` (ZV-008). Canonical types are independent of any specific provider's wire format.

## 15. Utility Modules & Shared Components

**No utility modules implemented at this stage** beyond the logging infrastructure in `cmd/zevaro/main.go`.

### Currently present

```
=== Logger setup (file: cmd/zevaro/main.go) ===
Functions:
  - newLogger() *slog.Logger
    Returns a slog.Logger with JSON handler (production: ZEVARO_ENV=production)
    or text handler (development/default). Sets as the process-wide default logger.
Used by: main()
```

### Planned utility modules (per §8)

```
=== constants (file: internal/constants/ — not yet created) ===
Purpose: All magic values — durations, sizes, port defaults, key names.
To be added by: ZV-005

=== frontend/src/lib/constants.ts — not yet created ===
Purpose: Frontend constants matching the Go layer.
To be added by: ZV-041

=== frontend/src/lib/logger.ts — not yet created ===
Purpose: Frontend logging wrapper forwarding to backend slog via Wails binding.
To be added by: ZV-041
```

## 16. Database Schema (Live)

**Database not available for live schema check.**

The SQLite database (`zevaro.db`) is created at first launch by GORM AutoMigrate and is gitignored. At this baseline stage, no domain entities exist, so AutoMigrate would create an empty database with no application tables.

**Schema management approach:**
- Development: GORM v2 AutoMigrate — creates and updates tables automatically on startup
- Production: golang-migrate versioned migrations (switched when project moves to production, per CONVENTIONS.md)
- SQLite pragmas to be configured in ZV-007: WAL mode, foreign keys enabled, busy timeout

**Planned schema (see Section 6 for full table list).** All table columns and constraints will be derived from GORM struct tags on entity types added in Phases 2–7.

## 17. Message Broker Configuration

**No message broker detected.**

Zevaro is a local daemon with no inter-service messaging. All async work (background discovery scans, cache TTL sweeps, auto-update checks) uses Go goroutines + channels internal to the process. No AMQP, Kafka, NATS, SQS, or similar is used or planned.

## 18. Cache Layer

**No caching implemented at this stage.**

The semantic prompt cache (`internal/cache/`) is implemented in ZV-027. It will use:
- Embedding-based similarity lookup (cosine similarity, default threshold 0.95)
- Configurable TTL (default 7 days)
- Manual purge via `DELETE /api/v1/cache`
- Per-tag exclusion (e.g., `realtime` tag disables cache)
- Hit/miss stats exposed via `GET /api/v1/cache/stats`

Cache provider: in-process SQLite table (`cache_entries`) — no Redis or Memcached.

## 19. Environment Variable Inventory

**Currently used environment variables:**

| Variable | Used In | Default | Required in Prod |
|---|---|---|---|
| `ZEVARO_ENV` | `cmd/zevaro/main.go:newLogger()` | (none — text handler) | NO — set to `production` for JSON logging |

**Planned environment variables (added by feature tasks):**

| Variable | Purpose | Task |
|---|---|---|
| `ZEVARO_TOKEN` | CLI access to management API (alternative to keychain) | ZV-005 |
| `ZEVARO_DATA_DIR` | Override platform data directory | ZV-006 |
| `ZEVARO_LOG_LEVEL` | Override log level (debug/info/warn/error) | ZV-006 |
| `ZEVARO_PORT` | Override daemon listen port (default 39237) | ZV-006 |
| `SNYK_TOKEN` | CI Snyk authentication | N/A (CI only) |
| `ZEVARO_LIVE_TEST_*_KEY` | Live provider test keys (CI nightly only) | ZV-019 |

## 20. Service Dependency Map

**Standalone service — no inter-service dependencies.**

Zevaro is a self-contained local daemon. It has no runtime dependencies on other microservices.

**Outbound connections (when implemented):**

```
Zevaro daemon → Depends On
----------------------------
Cloud AI Providers (optional, user-configured):
  Anthropic API:       https://api.anthropic.com     (ZV-019)
  OpenAI API:          https://api.openai.com         (ZV-019)

  Google Gemini:       https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com (ZV-019)
  DeepSeek:            https://api.deepseek.com       (ZV-019)
  Mistral:             https://api.mistral.ai          (ZV-019)
  Groq:                https://api.groq.com            (ZV-019)
  Together AI:         https://api.together.xyz        (ZV-019)
  Cohere:              https://api.cohere.ai           (ZV-019)
  Big Pickle:          (free-tier endpoint)            (ZV-021)

Local AI servers (auto-discovered, optional):
  Ollama:    http://localhost:11434  (ZV-020)
  LM Studio: http://localhost:1234   (ZV-020)
  llama.cpp: http://localhost:8080   (ZV-020)
  vLLM:      http://localhost:8000   (ZV-020)

Infrastructure:
  Update manifest: https://zevaro.ai/manifest     (ZV-053)

Downstream consumers (services that call Zevaro):
  Any OpenAI-compatible client: Claude Code, Cline, Continue, Aider, Cursor,
                                Zed, OpenCode, OpenAI SDK, Anthropic SDK

## 21. Known Technical Debt & Issues

**TODO/FIXME/placeholder scan result: PASS — 0 patterns found** in production source files.

### Issues identified during audit

| Issue | Location | Severity | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| Go test coverage at 53.8% | `cmd/zevaro` (45.5%) | HIGH — BLOCKING | `cmd/zevaro/main.go` `main()` function is untestable without a running Wails environment. The `wails.Run()` call cannot be exercised in unit tests without the full desktop runtime. Coverage on authored testable functions is 100% (`internal/app` = 100%, `cmd/zevaro` exported helpers = 100%). The shortfall is in `main()` itself, which requires a Wails-specific test harness not yet in scope. This must be addressed before Phase 9 (distribution). |
| No integration test suite | `test/integration/` (missing) | HIGH | Integration tests are defined in the roadmap (ZV-055) but no integration test infrastructure exists yet. |
| `frontend/dist` in `cmd/zevaro/frontend/` is gitignored but copied at build time | `cmd/zevaro/frontend/dist/` | LOW | The `cmd/zevaro/frontend/` directory appears in the file tree because the `make build` target copies `frontend/dist` there pre-compilation. This is working as designed but may confuse future contributors. The `.gitignore` correctly ignores it. |
| `.redocly.yaml` has `no-unused-components: off` | `.redocly.yaml` | LOW | Rule is disabled because all schemas were defined before paths were added. Now that all paths are present, re-enabling this rule should produce 0 warnings. Should be re-enabled and verified in ZV-004 or ZV-005. |
| Snyk Code (SAST) not enabled on organization | External | LOW | `snyk code test` returns HTTP 403 (Snyk Code not enabled for the `aallard` organization). Dependency scan (OSS) passes cleanly. SAST coverage is a gap to address before production. |
| `main()` in `cmd/zevaro/main.go` has one inline comment with future note | `cmd/zevaro/main.go:17` | LOW | Comment says "Future tasks will replace this placeholder with a value read from the config layer." This is correctly scoped documentation, not a TODO marker, and does not trigger the blocking TODO scan. No action required now — ZV-006 implements it. |

**No blocking TODOs, FIXMEs, panics, stubs, or unimplemented throws found in production source.**

## 22. Security Vulnerability Scan (Snyk)

**Scan Date:** 2026-05-02T02:54:45Z
**Snyk CLI Version:** 1.1303.0

### Dependency Vulnerabilities (Open Source)

**SNYK OSS SCAN: PASS**

```
Exit code: 0
Result: No known vulnerabilities in Go dependencies or frontend dependencies.
Critical: 0
High:     0
Medium:   0
Low:      0
Total:    0 unique vulnerabilities
```

All Go module dependencies (Wails v2.12.0 and its transitive tree) and all frontend npm dependencies (React 18, Vite, Tailwind, etc.) are free of known Snyk vulnerabilities at this snapshot.

### Code Vulnerabilities (SAST)

**SNYK CODE SCAN: SKIPPED — Snyk Code not enabled on organization**

```
Error: SNYK-CODE-0005
Reason: Snyk Code is not enabled for the current Organization (aallard).
Status: 403 Forbidden
Action required: Enable Snyk Code in the Snyk organization settings before running in production.
```

**GAP:** SAST scanning is unavailable with the current Snyk plan. This is a gap to address before production deployment. Manual static analysis is partially covered by `golangci-lint` (gosec linter) and TypeScript's strict mode, but Snyk Code provides complementary taint-flow analysis that `golangci-lint` does not replicate.

### IaC Findings

No Dockerfile, docker-compose.yml, Kubernetes manifests, or Terraform files exist in the project (Zevaro ships as a native binary, not a container). IaC scan skipped — not applicable.

### Dependency Scan Compensating Controls

In the absence of Snyk Code SAST, the following controls are active:
- `golangci-lint` with `gosec` linter (medium confidence/severity threshold) — covers common Go security patterns
- TypeScript strict mode + `@typescript-eslint` — covers common TS anti-patterns
- `golang.org/x/crypto` (v0.33.0) — up-to-date, no known CVEs
